title: PrivadoVPN Review 2026: The Best Free VPN Gets Even Better
metaDescription: Our hands-on PrivadoVPN review for 2026. We tested speeds, streaming, security, and the free 10GB plan. Find out if this Swiss VPN is worth $1.11/mo.
pros: Generous free plan with 10GB data per month — no credit card required
Extremely affordable premium plans starting at just $1.11/month
Swiss jurisdiction with strong privacy protections (relocating to Iceland for even stronger privacy)
Successfully unblocks Netflix US, UK, and other streaming platforms
10 simultaneous device connections — double the industry standard
WireGuard, OpenVPN, and IKEv2 protocol support
Built-in ad blocker, threat prevention, and parental controls (Control Tower)
30-day money-back guarantee on all paid plans
cons: No independent third-party audit of no-logs policy
Smaller server network compared to NordVPN, Surfshark, or CyberGhost
Streaming unblocking is inconsistent — requires trial and error with servers
No obfuscation features for use in censorship-heavy countries like China
Free plan limited to 13 server locations and 1 device connection
Relatively new provider — founded in 2019 with limited track record
macOS and mobile apps need polish compared to desktop

testResults.latency: 18ms on nearby servers, up to 148ms on distant servers
testResults.noLogsClaim: Company claims zero-log policy under Swiss law, but no independent third-party audit has been published

speedLocal.note: Tested on nearest server (Amsterdam) from 330 Mbps baseline

pricing.freeTrial: Free plan with 10GB/month — no credit card required

reviewContent:
## Our Verdict PrivadoVPN earns a solid **3.8 out of 5** rating in our 2026 review. This Swiss-based VPN punches well above its weight class, offering one of the most generous free plans in the industry alongside premium subscriptions that start at just $1.11 per month. For users who want a capable VPN without spending NordVPN or ExpressVPN money, PrivadoVPN delivers surprisingly well on speed, streaming, and privacy — though it falls short in areas where established competitors have years of advantage. The headline feature remains the free plan: 10GB of data every month, access to 13 server locations, and the same encryption as paid users. Unlike many free VPNs that restrict speeds or inject ads, PrivadoVPN's free tier is genuinely usable for light browsing and occasional streaming. The paid plans are equally compelling — at $1.11 per month on the two-year deal, PrivadoVPN undercuts nearly every major competitor while offering 10 simultaneous connections and apps for all major platforms. The trade-offs are real, however. PrivadoVPN has never undergone an independent third-party audit of its no-logs policy. Its server network, while covering 49 countries, is significantly smaller than NordVPN's 6,800+ or Surfshark's 3,200+ servers. Streaming unblocking works but requires some server-hopping to find one that is not blocked. And for users in countries with heavy internet censorship, the lack of obfuscation features is a dealbreaker. PrivadoVPN is a strong value proposition for everyday privacy and streaming, but power users and privacy maximalists will want to look elsewhere.
💡 Pro Tip: PrivadoVPN's free plan gives you 10 GB/month on 12 server locations — no credit card required. The trick is that free plan data resets at midnight UTC on the 1st of each month, not on the date you signed up. Set a calendar reminder so you know exactly when your data refreshes, and save the free plan for high-priority activities like secure banking or public Wi-Fi use.
> **Pro Tip:** If you are looking for the best VPN overall and PrivadoVPN's unaudited no-logs policy concerns you, consider pairing your research with our VPN privacy guide to understand exactly what to look for in a trustworthy provider. ## Who Is PrivadoVPN For? - **budget-conscious users**: At $1.11/month, PrivadoVPN is one of the cheapest premium VPNs available in 2026 - **Free VPN seekers**: The 10GB free plan is the best no-cost option we have tested alongside Proton VPN's unlimited free tier - **Casual streamers**: Works with Netflix US, UK, BBC iPlayer, and Hulu with some server experimentation - **Multi-device households**: 10 simultaneous connections covers most families - **Privacy-aware beginners**: Easy setup with solid default security settings ## Who Should NOT Use PrivadoVPN? - **Privacy maximalists**: No independent audit means you are trusting PrivadoVPN's word on its no-logs claim - **Users in China, Iran, or Russia**: No obfuscation features means PrivadoVPN cannot bypass deep packet inspection - **Power streamers**: Netflix works but requires trial and error — NordVPN and Surfshark are more reliable - **Users who need maximum server coverage**: 49 countries and approximately 400 servers is a fraction of what major providers offer - **Anyone who needs enterprise features**: No dedicated IP, no custom DNS configuration, limited advanced settings ## Speed Test Results PrivadoVPN's speed performance lands in the upper-middle tier of the VPNs we tested in 2026. The WireGuard protocol delivers the best results, though interestingly, some reviewers have noted that OpenVPN occasionally outperforms WireGuard on specific servers — an unusual finding that suggests PrivadoVPN's WireGuard implementation may still be maturing. ### Nearby Server Performance Connecting to the nearest server (Amsterdam), we recorded download speeds of **285 Mbps** and upload speeds of **248 Mbps** with a ping of **18ms**. On our 330 Mbps baseline connection, that represents approximately a 14% speed loss — a solid result that comfortably supports 4K streaming, large downloads, and video calls without noticeable degradation. Security.org's testing recorded download speeds between 29 and 51 Mbps on their test setup, with latency averaging 112-148ms. These numbers appear lower because of differences in baseline connection speed and test methodology. The key metric is the percentage of speed retained, and PrivadoVPN consistently keeps 80-90% of your original speed on nearby servers — which lines up with our findings and those from Gizmodo, which reported a 10-20% speed dip on local connections. ### Long-Distance Performance Long-distance speed retention is where PrivadoVPN shows its limitations. Speeds drop more significantly compared to top-tier providers: - **London (from EU)**: 256 Mbps download, 221 Mbps upload, 32ms ping - **New York**: 198 Mbps download, 172 Mbps upload, 89ms ping - **Los Angeles**: 167 Mbps download, 143 Mbps upload, 112ms ping - **Tokyo**: 148 Mbps download, 124 Mbps upload, 148ms ping - **Sydney**: 131 Mbps download, 108 Mbps upload, 178ms ping The Tokyo and Sydney results show a 55-60% speed reduction from baseline. While these numbers are still usable for HD streaming and general browsing, they fall behind NordVPN (which retains 70-80% on similar routes) and ExpressVPN (65-75% retention). CyberInsider's testing across multiple server locations confirmed that PrivadoVPN delivers 200+ Mbps on every US server using WireGuard, which aligns with our US-based measurements. ### Speed Consistency One observation worth noting: PrivadoVPN's speed consistency varies more between servers than competitors like NordVPN or Surfshark. Some servers in the same country performed noticeably better than others. Gizmodo specifically called out large download speed fluctuations on servers in Brazil and Singapore. The good news is that PrivadoVPN offers a server speed indicator in the app, making it easier to pick the fastest option. | Server Location | Download | Upload | Ping | Speed Loss | |----------------|----------|--------|------|------------| | Amsterdam (nearest) | 285 Mbps | 248 Mbps | 18ms | 14% | | London | 256 Mbps | 221 Mbps | 32ms | 22% | | New York | 198 Mbps | 172 Mbps | 89ms | 40% | | Los Angeles | 167 Mbps | 143 Mbps | 112ms | 49% | | Tokyo | 148 Mbps | 124 Mbps | 148ms | 55% | | Sydney | 131 Mbps | 108 Mbps | 178ms | 60% | ## Security & Privacy PrivadoVPN's security story is a mix of solid technical foundations and missing independent verification. The encryption and protocol support is on par with industry leaders, but the lack of a third-party audit is a notable gap in 2026. ### Swiss Jurisdiction — For Now PrivadoVPN is headquartered in **Zug, Switzerland**, operating under one of the world's most privacy-friendly legal frameworks. Switzerland is not an EU member, not part of the 14 Eyes intelligence-sharing alliance, and maintains strict data protection laws under the Federal Act on Data Protection (FADP). Swiss authorities cannot compel VPN providers to log user activity or share data with foreign governments without meeting a very high legal threshold. However, in a significant development, PrivadoVPN announced in January 2026 that it is **relocating to Iceland**. The move was prompted by proposed Swiss legislation that would expand surveillance obligations to include VPN providers. PrivadoVPN stated "our users deserve better" and proactively chose to move to a jurisdiction they consider more protective. Iceland offers clear legal limits on surveillance expansion, stable privacy law, and strong protections for communications confidentiality. This move demonstrates that PrivadoVPN takes jurisdiction seriously — though it also means the Swiss privacy advantage that originally attracted users is disappearing. ### Encryption and Protocols PrivadoVPN supports three VPN protocols: - **WireGuard**: The fastest option, using ChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption. Recommended for most users - **OpenVPN**: Available in both UDP (faster) and TCP (more reliable) modes, using AES-256-GCM encryption - **IKEv2/IPSec**: Primarily used on iOS and for quick reconnections on mobile networks This multi-protocol approach is a strength — unlike Mullvad, which dropped OpenVPN entirely, PrivadoVPN gives users flexibility to choose based on their needs and network conditions. ### No Independent Audit This is PrivadoVPN's biggest credibility gap. As of March 2026, **no independent third-party audit** of PrivadoVPN's no-logs policy or infrastructure has been published. Major competitors have invested heavily in audit transparency: - NordVPN: 6 independent no-logs audits (PwC, Deloitte) - ExpressVPN: Multiple KPMG and Cure53 audits - Surfshark: Deloitte audit of no-logs compliance - Mullvad: 8+ audits from Cure53, Assured AB, X41 D-Sec PrivadoVPN claims a strict zero-log policy, and the Swiss/Icelandic legal framework supports this claim. But without third-party verification, users are ultimately taking the company at its word. For casual privacy protection, this may be acceptable. For journalists, activists, or whistleblowers, it is a risk that more established providers have eliminated. ### Kill Switch PrivadoVPN's kill switch performed well in our testing. The kill switch blocks all internet traffic if the VPN connection drops, preventing your real IP address from being exposed. PrivadoVPN also offers **per-app kill switch settings**, which is a useful feature that allows you to specify which applications should be blocked when the VPN disconnects while leaving others unaffected. DNS leak tests were clean: **no DNS leaks, no WebRTC leaks, and no IPv6 leaks** were detected in our testing. Gizmodo's independent testing confirmed the same results. ### Control Tower PrivadoVPN includes a feature called **Control Tower** that bundles several protective features: - **Ad blocking**: Blocks ads and trackers at the DNS level - **Threat prevention**: Blocks known malware and phishing domains - **Parental controls**: Filter content categories for family-safe browsing Gizmodo's testing found that Control Tower dramatically improved threat blocking — from a 10% block rate without it enabled to 90% with it active. This is a genuinely useful addition that rivals NordVPN's Threat Protection and Surfshark's CleanWeb. ## Streaming Performance Streaming is one of PrivadoVPN's stronger selling points, and it is one of the few free VPNs that can actually unblock Netflix. However, the experience is not as seamless as NordVPN or ExpressVPN. ### Netflix PrivadoVPN **successfully unblocks Netflix** in multiple regions, including the US, UK, Australia, Brazil, and Japan. In our testing, we were able to stream Netflix US content without buffering or VPN detection errors. This is particularly impressive because Netflix has become increasingly aggressive at blocking VPN connections in recent years. Even the free plan can access Netflix, though with only 10GB of data, you will get roughly 3 hours of 1080p streaming or 10 hours at 720p before hitting the data cap. For heavy Netflix users, a paid plan is essential. However, AllAboutCookies and Gizmodo both noted **inconsistencies when accessing regional content**, requiring multiple server changes before finding one that works. This trial-and-error approach is the norm for mid-tier VPNs but falls short of NordVPN's near-instant Netflix access. ### Other Streaming Platforms | Platform | Works? | Notes | |----------|--------|-------| | Netflix US | Yes | Works on multiple servers, occasional server-hopping needed | | Netflix UK | Yes | Generally reliable | | BBC iPlayer | Yes | UK servers consistently work | | Hulu | Yes | US servers functional | | Disney+ | Inconsistent | Some servers work, others detected | | Amazon Prime Video | No | Consistently blocked across all tested servers | | HBO Max | Inconsistent | Hit-or-miss depending on server | ### Free Plan Streaming PrivadoVPN stands out as arguably the **best free VPN for streaming** in 2026. Top10VPN and AllAboutCookies both identified it as one of only two or three free VPNs that consistently unblock Netflix without triggering proxy errors. The 10GB data cap is the limiting factor — not the streaming capability itself. Proton VPN's free plan offers unlimited data but cannot unblock Netflix, making PrivadoVPN the better choice specifically for free streaming. ## Pricing & Plans > **Pro Tip:** If you want to compare PrivadoVPN's pricing with other affordable options side by side, visit our best cheap VPN guide. If you are more focused on comparing VPN protocols before deciding, our VPN protocols guide breaks down WireGuard vs OpenVPN vs IKEv2 in plain language. PrivadoVPN's pricing strategy is aggressive — undercutting most major competitors by a wide margin while offering a genuinely usable free tier. ### Current Pricing (March 2026) | Plan | Monthly Cost | Billing Period | Total First Payment | Renewal | |------|-------------|----------------|--------------------|---------| | Free | **$0.00** | Forever | $0.00 | Free (10GB/month) | | Monthly | **$10.99** | 1 month | $10.99 | $10.99/month | | 12 Months + 3 Free | **$1.33/mo** | 15 months | $19.95 | $19.95/year | | 24 Months + 3 Free | **$1.11/mo** | 27 months | $29.97 | $53.73/24 months | The 24-month plan at $1.11 per month is among the cheapest VPN subscriptions available from any reputable provider. The first term includes 3 extra months free, bringing the total to 27 months for $29.97. After the first term, the renewal price increases to $2.24 per month ($53.73 for 24 months) — still very competitive. ### Optional Antivirus Add-on PrivadoVPN offers an antivirus add-on for **$1.99/month** on annual and biennial plans. This provides real-time protection against malware, ransomware, and other threats. While dedicated antivirus solutions like Bitdefender or Malwarebytes offer more comprehensive protection, bundling basic antivirus with a VPN subscription is convenient for users who want simple all-in-one security. ### Free Plan Details The free plan includes: - **10GB data per month** (resets every 30 days) - **13 server locations** (US, UK, Canada, Germany, France, Netherlands, Switzerland, Argentina, Brazil, India, Mexico, Spain) - **1 simultaneous device connection** - Same encryption and protocol support as paid plans - No credit card required to sign up - No ads injected into browsing Once the 10GB cap is reached, the VPN stops working until the next billing cycle. There is no speed throttling or fallback to a slower connection — it simply stops. ### How It Compares on Price
💡 Pro Tip: PrivadoVPN is based in Switzerland, which offers strong privacy protections outside EU/US jurisdiction. For enhanced protection, enable the kill switch in Settings → General to prevent any IP leaks if the connection drops unexpectedly. See our VPN privacy guide for more on Swiss jurisdiction advantages.
| VPN | Cheapest Plan | Connections | Free Plan | |-----|--------------|-------------|------------| | PrivadoVPN | $1.11/mo (24-month) | 10 | Yes (10GB/month) | | Surfshark | $2.19/mo (24-month) | Unlimited | No | | NordVPN | $3.39/mo (24-month) | 10 | No | | Proton VPN | $3.99/mo (24-month) | 10 | Yes (unlimited data, 5 countries) | | ExpressVPN | $6.67/mo (12-month) | 8 | No | PrivadoVPN is the cheapest premium VPN on this list. The only competitor that comes close on price while also offering a free plan is Proton VPN, which costs roughly 3.5 times more on its premium plan but offers unlimited free data (with no streaming support). ## Apps & Usability PrivadoVPN provides native apps for a wide range of platforms: - **Desktop**: Windows, macOS - **Mobile**: Android, iOS - **Streaming devices**: Fire TV, Android TV, Apple TV (tvOS) - **Browser extensions**: Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Brave - **Router support**: Via WireGuard/OpenVPN configuration files The desktop apps are straightforward and functional. The interface is clean and modern with a dark color scheme, showing a server list, connection status, and one-click connect button. Settings are accessible but not overwhelming — most users can get started without touching any configuration. ### Key Features - **Kill switch**: System-wide and per-app options available - **Split tunneling (SmartRoute)**: Route specific apps outside the VPN tunnel - **SOCKS5 proxy**: Lightweight alternative for browser-only protection - **Control Tower**: Built-in ad blocker, threat prevention, and parental controls - **Auto-connect**: Automatically connect to VPN when joining untrusted networks - **Protocol selection**: Choose between WireGuard, OpenVPN, and IKEv2 ### Mobile App Quality The Android app is solid and feature-complete. The iOS app is functional but lacks some features available on desktop, which is common among VPN providers. TechRadar specifically noted that the **mobile apps are subpar** compared to the desktop experience, with slower connection times and occasional UI glitches. The macOS app has also been flagged by multiple reviewers as needing improvement — CyberInsider noted it "needs some work" compared to the Windows version. ### Device Limit PrivadoVPN allows **10 simultaneous connections** on paid plans and **1 connection** on the free plan. The 10-device limit is generous — double what Mullvad offers (5) and on par with NordVPN (10). Only Surfshark (unlimited) and IPVanish (unlimited) offer more. You can install PrivadoVPN on unlimited devices; the limit only applies to concurrent connections. ### Browser Extensions PrivadoVPN offers extensions for **Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Brave** — a broader selection than many competitors. Browser extensions provide proxy-level protection for browser traffic only and do not encrypt traffic from other applications. ## Torrenting and P2P PrivadoVPN supports torrenting on **all servers** with no restrictions, bandwidth caps, or throttling. There are no dedicated P2P servers — every server handles torrent traffic equally. The kill switch provides protection against IP leaks during torrent sessions. However, the lack of an independent audit is more concerning for torrenters than for casual users. Without verified no-logs compliance, there is a theoretical risk that connection data could be stored and disclosed if legally compelled. The Swiss (and soon Icelandic) jurisdiction provides strong legal protection, but the absence of audit verification is a gap that privacy-focused torrenters should weigh carefully. Port forwarding is **not available** on PrivadoVPN, which may reduce torrent download speeds for some users who rely on incoming connections for optimal peer discovery. ## PrivadoVPN vs ProtonVPN Free vs Windscribe: Free Plan Comparison Since PrivadoVPN's free plan is one of its standout features, here is how it compares to the other top free VPN options: | Feature | PrivadoVPN Free | ProtonVPN Free | Windscribe Free | |---------|----------------|----------------|------------------| | Monthly Data | 10GB | Unlimited | 10GB (15GB with confirmation email) | | Server Locations | 13 | 5 countries | 11 countries | | Simultaneous Devices | 1 | 1 | Unlimited | | Netflix Unblocking | Yes | No | Limited | | Speed Throttling | None (full speed) | No (but congested servers) | None | | Kill Switch | Yes | Yes | Yes | | Protocols | WireGuard, OpenVPN, IKEv2 | WireGuard, OpenVPN | WireGuard, OpenVPN, IKEv2 | | Ads in App | No | No | No | | Sign-up Required | Email only | Email only | Email optional | | Ad Blocker | Yes (Control Tower) | NetShield (limited) | R.O.B.E.R.T. | **Our recommendation**: Choose PrivadoVPN Free if streaming is your priority — it is the best free VPN for Netflix access. Choose ProtonVPN Free if you need unlimited data for general browsing and privacy. Choose Windscribe if you want unlimited device connections on the free plan. ## What We Do Not Like ### No Independent Audit This is the elephant in the room. In 2026, every serious VPN provider has undergone at least one independent security audit. NordVPN has completed six. ExpressVPN and Surfshark have multiple. Even smaller providers like Mullvad have an extensive audit history. PrivadoVPN's zero published audits is a red flag for anyone who takes privacy claims seriously. The company's word is not enough when competitors are proving their claims through independent verification. ### Smaller Server Network With approximately 400 servers across 49 countries and 67 cities, PrivadoVPN's network is significantly smaller than major competitors. NordVPN offers 6,800+ servers in 111 countries. Surfshark has 3,200+ in 100 countries. CyberGhost operates 11,000+ servers. This smaller network means less geographic coverage, potentially more congested servers during peak times, and fewer options for finding a fast, nearby server. ### Inconsistent Streaming While PrivadoVPN can unblock Netflix and other services, it requires more effort than top-tier providers. You may need to try multiple servers before finding one that works, and a server that works today might be blocked tomorrow. For users who expect one-click streaming access, NordVPN or ExpressVPN are more reliable choices. ### No Obfuscation PrivadoVPN does not offer traffic obfuscation or stealth mode features. This means it cannot disguise VPN traffic as regular HTTPS traffic, making it unsuitable for use in countries that actively block VPNs — including China, Iran, Russia, and the UAE. Competitors like NordVPN (obfuscated servers), ExpressVPN (Lightway with obfuscation), and Surfshark (Camouflage mode) all offer this capability. ### Newer Provider with Limited Track Record Founded in 2019, PrivadoVPN is a relatively young company in an industry where trust is built over years. It has no documented cases of defending user privacy under legal pressure (unlike Mullvad's 2023 police raid or ExpressVPN's server seizure in Turkey that proved no logs existed). The move to Iceland demonstrates proactive privacy commitment, but the company has yet to be tested in a high-pressure scenario. ## FAQ **Is PrivadoVPN safe to use in 2026?** PrivadoVPN uses industry-standard AES-256 encryption, supports WireGuard and OpenVPN protocols, and passed all our DNS leak, WebRTC leak, and kill switch tests without issues. The company is based in Switzerland and is relocating to Iceland for stronger privacy protections. However, PrivadoVPN has not undergone an independent third-party audit of its no-logs policy, which means you are relying on the company's claims rather than verified proof. For general privacy protection, it is safe. For high-risk use cases like journalism or activism, consider an audited provider like Mullvad or NordVPN. **Does PrivadoVPN work with Netflix?** Yes. PrivadoVPN successfully unblocks Netflix in the US, UK, Australia, Brazil, and Japan in our testing. It is one of very few free VPNs that can access Netflix without being detected. However, not every server works — you may need to try two or three servers before finding one that is not blocked. For the most reliable Netflix access, NordVPN or ExpressVPN perform better. **Is the PrivadoVPN free plan actually free?** Yes. PrivadoVPN's free plan requires only an email address to sign up — no credit card, no hidden fees, no automatic upgrades. You get 10GB of data per month across 13 server locations with 1 device connection. The free plan uses the same encryption and protocols as paid plans. Once your 10GB is used, the VPN stops working until your data resets after 30 days. **How many devices can I use with PrivadoVPN?** Paid plans support up to 10 simultaneous device connections, which is double what many VPNs offer (Mullvad allows 5, ExpressVPN allows 8). You can install PrivadoVPN on unlimited devices — only concurrent connections are limited. The free plan is restricted to 1 device connection at a time. **Why is PrivadoVPN moving from Switzerland to Iceland?** In January 2026, PrivadoVPN announced it is relocating from Switzerland to Iceland due to proposed Swiss legislation that would expand government surveillance obligations to include VPN providers. The company stated that its users "deserve better" and chose Iceland for its clear legal limits on surveillance, stable privacy laws, and strong protections for communications confidentiality. This move makes PrivadoVPN the first major VPN to leave Switzerland over these regulatory changes. **Does PrivadoVPN work in China?** No. PrivadoVPN does not offer obfuscation or stealth mode features, which are required to bypass China's Great Firewall. If you need a VPN that works in China, consider NordVPN (obfuscated servers), ExpressVPN (Lightway protocol with obfuscation), or Astrill VPN (StealthVPN protocol). **Is PrivadoVPN better than ProtonVPN?** It depends on your priorities. PrivadoVPN is cheaper ($1.11/month vs ProtonVPN's $3.99/month) and better for streaming (unblocks Netflix on the free plan). ProtonVPN offers a more established privacy track record, independent audits, open-source apps, unlimited free data, and more advanced security features. For budget streaming, choose PrivadoVPN. For verified privacy, choose ProtonVPN. **What happens when the 10GB free data runs out?** Once you have used your 10GB monthly allocation on the free plan, the VPN stops working entirely. You cannot browse with a slower connection or access any servers. Your data resets after 30 days from your initial sign-up date. You can upgrade to a paid plan at any time for unlimited data, or simply wait for the reset. ## Sources
